g.text: Associating the JDBC/ODBC Bridge with the Database

rect.title: Introduction
rect.text: In this section, we will explore how to associate the JDBC/ODBC bridge with a database.
The JDBC/ODBC bridge is a bridge between the JDBC API and ODBC (Open Database Connectivity) drivers.

rect.title: What is JDBC?
rect.text: JDBC (Java Database Connectivity) is an API for accessing databases from Java programs.
It is a standard API for accessing relational databases.

rect.title: Types of JDBC Drivers
rect.text: There are two types of JDBC drivers:

rect.data-narration: Associating the JDBC/ODBC Bridge with the Database
rect.title: Associating the JDBC/ODBC Bridge with the Database
rect.text: To associate the JDBC/ODBC bridge with a database, follow these steps:
Download and install the ODBC driver for your database.
Download and install the JDBC/ODBC bridge.
Import the necessary JDBC packages in your Java program.
Set the class path for the JDBC driver.
Create a connection to the database using the JDBC API.

rect.title: Example
rect.text: Here is an example of how to associate the JDBC/ODBC bridge with a database:
